title: 10 best herbal creams for phlegm

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with phlegm because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The active compound eucalyptol in Eucalyptus globulus oil can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This can provide relief from congestion and coughs associated with respiratory infections and allergies. The cream's cooling and anti-inflammatory effects can also help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat and chest, promoting a smoother breathing experience. Regular use can help manage symptoms and improve respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps break down and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm.
Bornyl acetate: It has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce congestion and promote the removal of phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Eucalyptol: It has mucolytic properties, which help dissolve and break down thick mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear from the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain eucalyptol, a primary component that helps to relieve congestion and break down phlegm.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues.
Oils from leaves: While not an exact part, the essential oil extracted from the leaves (Eucalyptus oil) is widely used in creams and ointments to help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 10g of beeswax in a clean workspace.
Infuse 100ml of carrier oil with 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for 2 hours.
Combine 100ml of infused carrier oil with 20g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 20-30 minutes or until fully melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with phlegm because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and thin mucus. By reducing inflammation in the airways, it allows for easier breathing and helps to expel phlegm. Additionally, glycyrrhizin's expectorant properties stimulate the body to produce more mucus, which can then be easily cleared out, providing relief from congestion and coughs associated with phlegm buildup. This natural approach offers a holistic solution for respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Licorinic acid: Helps to break down and reduce phlegm by inhibiting the production of mucus and reducing its viscosity, making it easier to expel.
Glycyrrhizin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and coughing.

Flavonoids (including isoorientin and isovitexin): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and the production of excess phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are used to make creams for phlegm because they contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to break down and eliminate mucus.
Roots: The root's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing phlegm production.
Roots: Additionally, the roots' expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to expel from the body.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and mix with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Add 50g of coconut oil and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well.
Continue heating the mixture at 180°f for 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to 140°f before pouring into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with phlegm because they contain compounds that stimulate the lymphatic system and boost the immune system. The active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help to thin and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. By reducing inflammation and congestion, these creams can alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote overall respiratory health. Regular use of Echinacea purpurea creams may also help to prevent the recurrence of phlegm-related issues. This natural remedy offers a soothing and non-invasive solution for respiratory wellness.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Helps with phlegm by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and congestion associated with excess mucus.
Cichoric acid: Contributes to phlegm reduction by displaying anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help combat underlying infections causing phlegm production.
Caffeic acid: May help with phlegm by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and the associated phlegm accumulation in the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in immunomodulatory compounds and alkaloids, which help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves for 7-10 days in a warm dark place.
Steep 10 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in 100ml of jojoba oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 50 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture at 180-190 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with phlegm because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract. The active compounds present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing congestion and promoting a healthy mucous environment. By reducing phlegm production and facilitating its expulsion, thymus vulgaris creams offer relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis, coughs, and colds, making them a popular natural remedy for respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacterial and viral infections that contribute to phlegm formation.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the airways.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make creams for phlegm due to their high thymol content, which has antimicrobial properties and helps to break down phlegm.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used to make creams for phlegm due to their rich content of thymol and other essential oils, which aid in loosening and clearing phlegm.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make creams for phlegm due to their high concentration of thymol and other volatile oils, which help to reduce congestion and promote expectoration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a well-maintained garden or indoor pot on a sunny day.
Steam distill the fresh thymus vulgaris leaves using 2 cups of water at 212 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the obtained thymus vulgaris essential oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Mix 2 tablespoons of shea butter with 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Blend the thymus vulgaris essential oil mixture with the shea butter mixture in a 1:1 ratio and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with phlegm because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural expectorant properties. This means it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also reduce swelling and congestion, further alleviating symptoms of phlegm buildup. By promoting the expulsion of phlegm, ginger creams provide relief from coughs, congestion, and chest tightness associated with respiratory infections and allergies.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break down and expel phlegm from the respiratory system.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which aid in reducing phlegm production and easing congestion.
Zingiberene: As a sesquiterpene, zingiberene exhibits expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving respiratory issues caused by excess phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain a significant amount of zingiberene, a compound that helps to break down and expel phlegm, making it a popular ingredient in creams for this purpose.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale contain a high concentration of gingerols, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory congestion, making them a valuable ingredient in creams for phlegm relief.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 cup of zingiber officinale root in 2 cups of carrier oil by macerating for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids after 2 weeks of maceration.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler to melt at 120-140°f for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of the zingiber officinale extract and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture in double boiler.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let them cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with phlegm because they contain menthol, a natural decongestant and expectorant. Menthol works by thinning and loosening mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. This can provide relief from congestion and coughs associated with respiratory infections, such as the common cold. By reducing the thickness and stickiness of phlegm, Mentha x piperita creams can also help to clear airways and promote a healthy cough, ultimately relieving discomfort and promoting overall respiratory well-being.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin and loosen phlegm by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to expel.
Linalool: Exhibits bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax airway muscles and improve breathing, reducing phlegm accumulation.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs by increasing the secretion of mucus and promoting its removal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their menthol content helps to thin and expel mucus, providing relief from congestion.
Stems: Like leaves, stems also contain menthol, making them useful for decongestant creams.

(Optional) Roots: Although less commonly used than leaves and stems, roots of Mentha x piperita may still be used to add a subtle, earthy flavor to creams while contributing to their decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the infused liquid to the beeswax mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30-45 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica creams helps with phlegm because of its unique properties. The herb is known for its ability to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body. The creams typically contain extracts of Ephedra sinica, which work to break down and reduce the viscosity of phlegm. This makes it easier to cough up, providing relief from congestion and respiratory issues. By using Ephedra sinica creams, individuals can help alleviate phlegm-related symptoms and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help reduce phlegm by inhibiting the production of mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear out excess phlegm from the airways.
Terpenoids: These compounds possess decongestant and bronchodilatory properties, which help relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Their ephedrine content helps to reduce mucus production and relieve respiratory issues.
Seeds: Rich in ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, they aid in decongesting the airways and reducing phlegm.
Stems: Containing ephedrine and other bioactive compounds, they help to thin mucus and facilitate its expulsion.
remedy_recipe: Combine 30g of dried ephedra sinica with 50g of base cream in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl at 30-second intervals for 2 minutes.
Add 5g of vegetable glycerin and 5g of beeswax to the warm mixture and stir until dissolved.
Remove the bowl from the heat source and let the mixture cool to around 40°c in 30 minutes.
Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, pour it into small containers and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with phlegm because of the herb's natural antitussive and expectorant properties. The active compounds present in Rosmarinus officinalis, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, work together to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. By thinning and loosening mucus, these creams facilitate its expulsion from the body, providing relief from phlegm and coughs associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions. This makes Rosmarinus officinalis creams a popular natural remedy for respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound helps reduce inflammation and congestion associated with phlegm, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus.
Carvacrol: This terpene has decongestant and expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear phlegm from the airways, relieving coughs and congestion.
Bornyl Acetate: This sesquiterpene has a bronchodilatory effect, widening airways and making it easier to breathe, while also helping to reduce phlegm production and expel it from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in creams due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to relieve phlegm and congestion.
Stems: Utilized in creams because of their ability to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits, which can aid in the treatment of phlegm-related respiratory issues.
Flowers: Employed in creams for their aromatic and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and clear the respiratory tract, reducing phlegm and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis flowers and leaves from a trusted source to ensure quality.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis in 2 cups of distilled water for 10 minutes to release oils.
Strain the infused liquid and combine it with the beeswax mixture in the double boiler.
Whip 8 ounces of sweet almond oil with 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to enhance moisturizing properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with phlegm because of its potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds in Salvia officinalis, such as tannins and rosmarinic acid, help to break down and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. This natural remedy soothes irritated membranes, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate phlegm production. As a result, Salvia officinalis creams can provide effective relief from respiratory issues, promoting a smoother and healthier breathing experience.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate respiratory issues associated with phlegm.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat the underlying oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to excessive phlegm production.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has bronchodilatory effects, which help relax the airway muscles and improve breathing, reducing congestion and phlegm buildup.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils, which are used to create anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties in creams.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve phlegm congestion.
Roots: The roots of the plant have been used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10-20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 70-80 degree celsius for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Add 10-20 grams of beeswax and 10-20 grams of candelilla wax to the infused oil mixture and melt the waxes in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture until it reaches a creamy consistency using an electric whisk or a hand mixer for 5-7 minutes.
Transfer the herbal salvia officinalis cream to clean glass jars and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with phlegm because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce congestion and promote the clearance of mucus from the airways. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba creams can also help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by phlegm buildup. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may improve blood flow to the lungs, further aiding in the removal of excess mucus and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and congestion associated with phlegm.
Bilobalide: This constituent has expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Ginkgolides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to phlegm formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba in creams for phlegm, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids with anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing mucus production.
Barks: The barks are sometimes used in combination with leaves, as they have additional anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in relieving respiratory issues.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, making them a potential ingredient in creams for phlegm relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 1 teaspoon of the ginkgo biloba powder to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and remove it from the heat after 10-15 minutes of preparation time.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and allow it to solidify for at least 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for phlegm are topical treatments that help alleviate excess mucus and congestion, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and asthma. These creams typically contain a blend of herbal extracts, including Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Ephedra sinica, Rosmarinus officinalis, Salvia officinalis, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, phlegm can lead to shortness of breath, chronic coughing, and fatigue, severely impacting daily life, work, and overall well-being.
